title: Module 1
content:
Clutch And Gear Box
Requirement of transmission system, Principle of operation of clutches,
classification, Construction of Single plate coil spring and Diaphragm spring
clutches, Multi plate clutch, Cone clutch, Semi centrifugal clutch , Centrifugal
clutch. Need and Objectives of Gear box. Construction and operation of
Sliding mesh, Constant mesh and Synchromesh gearboxes, CVT and DCT –
Simple problems on determination of gear ratios for vehicles.

title: Module 2
content:
Epicyclic Gearboxes and Application
Principle of Planetary gear trains – Ford – T-model gearbox, Wilson Gear box,
Electromagnetic transmission, Chevrolet
“Turboglide” Transmission,
Hydraulic control system for Automatic Transmission.

title: Module 3
content:
Hydrodynamic Transmission
Fluid coupling-Principle-Constructional details. Performance characteristics.
Reduction of drag torque in fluid coupling. Torque converter-Principle-
constructional details, performance characteristics. Multistage torque
converters and Polyphase torque converters.

title: Module 4
content:
Hydrostatic And Electric Drives
Hydrostatic drive; Various types of hydrostatic systems – Principles of
Hydrostatic drive system. Advantages and limitations. Comparison of
hydrostatic drive with hydrodynamic drive, construction and working of
typical Janny hydrostatic drive. Electric drive-types- Principle of early and
modified Ward Leonard Control system-Advantages & limitations.
